keyPoints: Waterborne diseases are caused by pathogens like bacteria, viruses, and parasites that are transmitted through contaminated water.
Examples of waterborne diseases include cholera, dysentery, and typhoid fever.
Water-induced diseases are caused by exposure to contaminated water and include conditions like skin infections, eye infections, and respiratory problems.
Factors that contribute to waterborne diseases include poor sanitation, inadequate wastewater treatment, and contaminated water sources.
Prevention measures include proper treatment and disposal of human waste, regular cleaning and disinfection of water sources, and education on safe water handling practices.
